# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

`AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'`
`Description: Create a security group`
`Parameters:`
    `KeyName`:
        Type: String
        Description: Key name of an existing EC2 key pair
        Default: demo-keypair
`Resources:`
    `VPCSecurityGroupEC2InstanceAccess`:
        Type: 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup' 
        Properties:
            GroupDescription: Allows SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80) and HTTPS (port 443). Allowing ingress is optional for production instances.
            VpcId: !Ref VPC
            GroupName: 'VPCSecurityGroupEC2InstanceAccess' # This value will be automatically generated when deployed.
`Outputs:`
    `VPCSecurityGroupEC2InstanceAccessGroupId`:
        Value: !Ref VPCSecurityGroupEC2InstanceAccess
        Description: The security group ID of the EC2 instance security group created for allowing access from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0).
        Export:
            Name: 'ECSInstanceSecurityGroup'

`End of code`
